ontologistAudit.note: This is the FIRST kosmos session that touches all 5 domains (SECURITY + LEARN are first-class, not N/A). palantir-mini's entire raison d'etre is to put LEARN on a durable footing (append-only events.jsonl) and give SECURITY a real home (CapabilityToken vault + Layer-1 RBAC + managed-settings.d/ allowlists).
ontologistAudit.keyInsight: palantir-mini cleanly maps Anthropic Managed Agents virtualization (Session = append-only log / Harness = stateless MCP bridge / Sandbox = worktree) onto Palantir 5-domain ontology with no fudge. The EventEnvelope discriminated union (10 variants) IS the LEARN-domain Golden Table, and every DATA/LOGIC/ACTION mutation flows through it as events. SECURITY rides on Native Runtime mechanics (disallowedTools, managed-settings.d/, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}) instead of new runtime code.
ontologistAudit.heavyLift: The transition-zone call for LinkType/Interface/DerivedProperty/Reducer -> LOGIC (not DATA) per Ontologist SH-01 + DC5-06 was applied rigorously. Every EditFunction returning Edits[] is LOGIC (SH-03). The commit wrapper that applies Edits[] atomically is ACTION. Submission criteria gates are SECURITY (who may commit under what conditions) AND LOGIC (they compute). Audit classification: SubmissionCriteria is modeled twice — once as LOGIC primitive (pre-flight evaluator function) and once as SECURITY policy (pre-flight access gate). This is intentional: the computation is LOGIC, the gate semantics is SECURITY.

data.name: Data Domain (What Exists)
data.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete LOGIC + ACTION. Does it still describe reality? Yes -> DATA.

primitives.name: EventEnvelope
primitives.description: The append-only Session log record. 10 variants via type discriminator. Immutable once appended. Every write to ontology state MUST flow through an EventEnvelope.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: EventEnvelope describes WHAT HAPPENED. Delete all edit-function logic, delete all commit actions — the events.jsonl still describes reality as a durable record. Classification: DATA.
primitives.optimisticConcurrency: EventEnvelopeBase.sequence (monotonic counter) serves as version vector — see gapsResolved.gap-01

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — EventEnvelope, sequence, appliedEdits match Anthropic + Palantir domain vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — ONE struct hosts all 10 event types via discriminated union; no per-type envelope duplication
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new event types add a new variant to the union without modifying existing variants (Open/Closed by discriminator)
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — emit_event (producer) extends the union; replay_lineage (consumer) uses super type via exhaustive visitor on type tag

forwardPropPath.chain: ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/lineage/event-types.ts (declaration) -> bun codegen -> lib/event-log/types.ts + per-project src/generated/events.d.ts -> plugin runtime emit_event -> <project>/.palantir-mini/session/events.jsonl

backwardPropPath.chain: events.jsonl -> replay_lineage MCP tool -> 5-dim lineage graph -> drift detection -> ontology refinement proposal -> ontologist review -> schemas/ontology/lineage/ update

primitives.name: ObjectType

primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: ObjectType describes what KIND of entity can exist (e.g. Spacekid, BeatRecord). Delete all traversal logic and all mutations — the type declaration still describes reality. Classification: DATA.

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — 'ObjectType' matches Palantir OSDK vocabulary exactly
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — single schema declaration shared by kosmos/palantir-math/mathcrew via codegen
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new ObjectTypes added as new declarations, not mutations of existing
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — ontology declarations (producer) extend; generated client (consumer super) reads via structural types

primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: PropertyType describes CONSTRAINED value shapes (string-regex, integer-range, enum, struct). Delete all logic — the shapes still describe reality. Classification: DATA.

primitives.name: SnapshotManifest
primitives.description: Derived snapshot metadata: version, atSequence (replay cut-point), generatedAt, sourceEventCount. Snapshots are cache, not truth — events.jsonl is truth.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Manifest describes what snapshot exists at which event sequence. Stored fact about cache state. Classification: DATA.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — single manifest shape across projects

primitives.description: Branded `string & { readonly __brand: 'CommitSha' }` for atopWhich lineage dimension. Nominal type prevents mixing with SessionId / EventId at compile time.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: A CommitSha is a stored fact — git's own DAG is external truth. Classification: DATA.

logic.name: Logic Domain (How To Reason)
logic.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Does it return Edits[] or commit? Returns Edits[] -> LOGIC. Describes a relationship/computation without executing -> LOGIC.

primitives.name: EditFunction
primitives.description: Tier-2 function that takes params and returns `Edits[]` WITHOUT committing. Per Palantir logic/functions.md LOGIC.FN-04: functions compute mutations, actions execute them. Mechanically enforced via disallowedTools: [Write, Edit] on the edit-function agent .md frontmatter (per OMC architect.md pattern).
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: returns Edits[] array, no side effects -> LOGIC. SH-01: it COMPUTES what should change, it does not CHANGE anything.
primitives.mechanicalEnforcement: plugin agents/edit-function.md declares disallowedTools: [Write, Edit, Bash]. Agent cannot physically commit — the runtime blocks it. This is the cleanest implementation of LOGIC/ACTION separation.

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — 'EditFunction' and 'returns Edits[]' match Palantir OSDK 2.0 + authoring helper (functions.md L496) vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one function-signature template serves all mutation kinds; no per-kind boilerplate
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new EditFunction types added as new declarations; existing functions never modified
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — EditFunction<Params, Edits extends OntologyEdit[]> — Edits param is covariant (producer extends), consumer uses `OntologyEdit[]` supertype

forwardPropPath.chain: schemas/ontology/functions/function-signature.ts -> codegen -> per-project src/generated/functions/*.ts -> plugin apply_edit_function MCP handler -> returns Edits[] WITHOUT commit

backwardPropPath.chain: edit_proposed event (containing hypotheticalEdits) -> events.jsonl -> replay_lineage -> audit which functions proposed which edits -> refine function logic if evaluation degrades

primitives.name: InterfaceType
primitives.description: Shared behavioral contract across 2+ ObjectTypes. Per ontologist.md Transition Zone: Interface is LOGIC not DATA — it enables polymorphic reasoning, not entity identity.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: Interface REASONS about shared behavior across object types. SH-02 Transition Zone rule: though it looks declarative, it is a reasoning construct -> LOGIC.
primitives.source: decision-log projectOntologyScope.DATA.primitives (reclassified here per SH-01 transition zone)
primitives.classificationNote: decision-log.json lists 'InterfaceType' under DATA primitives. This is a known transition-zone collision. Per ontologist.md line 68: 'Interface enables reasoning and graph traversal, not stored facts -> LOGIC.' The ontologist reclassifies to LOGIC. decision-log's DATA listing is tolerated because it only says the primitive 'exists' in that domain scope; the semantic home is LOGIC.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one Interface reused across object types instead of duplicating shared fields
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new ObjectType implements existing Interface without changing Interface
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — Interface<Props> is structurally covariant for consumers

primitives.name: LinkType / Object-Backed Link Type
primitives.description: Relationship between ObjectTypes. Plain LinkType when the relationship carries no properties; Object-Backed LinkType when it does (e.g. weighted, timestamped, annotated edges).
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-01 + ontologist.md Transition Zone: LinkType enables reasoning/traversal across objects. It is NOT a stored fact about one object. Classification: LOGIC.

primitives.classificationNote: Same transition-zone rationale as InterfaceType. decision-log lists LinkType under DATA; ontologist relocates to LOGIC per the authoritative transition zone rule.

primitives.name: DerivedProperty
primitives.description: Property computed from other properties. Not stored. Lives in schemas/ontology/functions/derived-property.ts.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: DerivedProperty REASONS over raw data to produce a value. SH-02: If you delete the computation, the raw data remains but the derived value vanishes — classification: LOGIC.

primitives.name: Reducer
primitives.description: Aggregation or fold over a collection of objects (e.g. total count of ObjectTypes per domain, sum of property values). Critical for snapshot derivation from events.jsonl.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: Reducer REASONS over a collection. No mutation, no side effect. Classification: LOGIC.
primitives.specialRole: Snapshot generation from events.jsonl is itself a reducer: Reducer<EventEnvelope, OntologySnapshot> that folds the log into current state. This is the DC5-06 primitive that powers LEARN -> DATA derivation.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one reducer contract serves snapshot generation, aggregation queries, and drift detection
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new reducers added without modifying existing
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — Reducer<In, Out> is classic PECS territory: In is consumer super, Out is producer extends

primitives.name: LinkConstraint
primitives.description: Computed constraint on a LinkType (e.g. cardinality, type-of-both-ends, temporal ordering). No side effects — computes a boolean or Constraint[].
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: returns Constraint[] or boolean, not edits -> LOGIC.

primitives.name: SubmissionCriteria (computational)
primitives.description: Pre-flight business-logic evaluator. Palantir action/mutations.md ACTION.MU-14..17 + OSDK 2.0 $validateOnly pattern. Returns Pass | Fail with 9 constraint classes (Range, ArraySize, StringLength, StringRegexMatch, OneOf, ObjectQueryResult, ObjectPropertyValue, GroupMember, Unevaluable).
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: submission criteria COMPUTE pass/fail, they do not commit. Classification: LOGIC. NOTE: the GATE SEMANTICS is modeled separately in SECURITY domain — see prim-sec-02. Intentional dual modeling.
primitives.source: decision-log RQ-06 + blueprint.md Gap fill 1 lines 359-365

primitives.description: 6-phase validation pipeline evaluator (Design/Compile/Deploy/Merge/Runtime/Post-Write per §VAL.R-03..R-08). v0 implements minimum 3: Design + Compile + Runtime. Each phase function returns Pass | Fail<ErrorClass>.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: phase evaluators COMPUTE pass/fail. They do not commit. Classification: LOGIC.

action.name: Action Domain (What Changes Reality)
action.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: commits edits, produces side effects -> ACTION. SH-01: CHANGES reality when executed.

primitives.name: Tier-1 Declarative Action
primitives.description: Pure CRUD rule. Declarative spec: 'given ObjectTypeX and field Y, on operation Z, apply rule R.' No custom code path. Compiled into commit_edits handler fast path. Per Palantir ACTION.MU-09..11: mutually exclusive with Tier-2.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-03 + SH-01: Tier-1 Action COMMITS edits — it changes reality. Classification: ACTION.
primitives.mutuallyExclusive: Tier-1 and Tier-2 are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE per Palantir ACTION.MU-09..11. An ActionType is one or the other, never both.

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — 'Tier-1 Declarative Action' matches Palantir vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one declarative rule schema serves all CRUD kinds
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new rules added via new declarations

forwardPropPath.chain: schemas/ontology/primitives/action-type.ts (tier-1 union member) -> codegen -> commit_edits MCP fast path -> edit_committed event -> events.jsonl

backwardPropPath.chain: edit_committed event (with actionTypeRid) -> events.jsonl -> replay_lineage -> which actions changed reality, when, by whom -> refinement

primitives.name: Tier-2 Function-Backed Action
primitives.description: Action type that wraps an EditFunction (LOGIC) with an atomic commit. The EditFunction computes Edits[] without committing; the Tier-2 Action takes those Edits[] and commits them atomically (with submission criteria pre-flight).
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: COMMITS edits via wrapping the LOGIC-domain function. The wrapping is the mutation. Classification: ACTION.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one Tier-2 wrapper serves all EditFunction types
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new EditFunctions get new Tier-2 wrappers without modifying existing
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — wrapper is `Action<F extends EditFunction<any, any>>` — F is producer-extends

forwardPropPath.chain: schemas/ontology/primitives/action-type.ts (tier-2) -> codegen -> commit_edits MCP handler slow path -> apply_edit_function (LOGIC) + AtomicCommit (ACTION) -> edit_committed event

backwardPropPath.chain: edit_committed event -> replay_lineage -> which function produced which edits -> both function and wrapper refinement

primitives.name: AtomicCommit
primitives.description: The actual mutation: writes edits to ontology state AND appends edit_committed event to events.jsonl in a single atomic operation. Uses proper-lockfile (or zero-dep fs.mkdir fallback) for mutual exclusion.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-03 + SH-01: AtomicCommit literally changes reality. Classification: ACTION.
primitives.atomicityMechanism: Per blueprint.md Gap fill 3 lines 388-394: proper-lockfile primary, fs.mkdir zero-dep fallback. mkdir is atomic on all filesystems — EEXIST on race. Exponential backoff retry loop.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one commit primitive serves Tier-1 fast path and Tier-2 slow path
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS

forwardPropPath.chain: commit_edits MCP handler -> AtomicCommit -> write ontology state + append events.jsonl with lockfile mutex

backwardPropPath.chain: events.jsonl -> replay_lineage -> verify atomicity invariants (no torn writes, sequence monotonic)

primitives.name: ActionExecutorAgent (callable)
primitives.description: Plugin subagent with Write/Edit/Bash allowlist. The ONLY palantir-mini agent permitted to commit. All other agents (edit-function, architect, verifier) have disallowedTools covering Write/Edit. Per OMC architect.md pattern + Correction 1 (plugin agents cannot define hooks).
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: this agent exists specifically to CHANGE reality via wrapped action execution. Classification: ACTION.
primitives.nativeRuntimeMechanism: agents/action-executor.md frontmatter: tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit]. Other agents: disallowedTools: [Write, Edit]. Runtime physically blocks non-executor agents from committing.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one executor agent serves all commit kinds; no per-type executor
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new action types added without modifying executor

forwardPropPath.chain: plugin agents/action-executor.md -> action-executor agent invocation -> calls commit_edits MCP -> AtomicCommit

backwardPropPath.chain: edit_committed event (byWhom.agentName='action-executor') -> replay_lineage -> verify only action-executor committed

primitives.name: CodegenRun
primitives.description: Descender regeneration action: triggered by PostToolUse(Edit schemas/ontology/*) hook. Reads ontology declarations, writes src/generated/* per project, appends codegen_started + codegen_completed events.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: generates files on disk. CHANGES reality. Classification: ACTION.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — single codegen entrypoint, not per-primitive
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new primitive types add to codegen via new template, not modification

forwardPropPath.chain: schemas/ontology/ -> PostToolUse hook -> CodegenRun -> src/generated/* + codegen_completed event

security.name: Security Domain (Who Governs Access)
security.semanticHeuristic: SH-04: Does this govern WHO can do something, or UNDER WHAT CONDITIONS an action is permitted? -> SECURITY.
security.note: palantir-mini v0 SECURITY is aggressively scoped. Layer-1 RBAC only — Markings, ObjectSecurity, PermissionsCascade deferred to future phase. v0 SECURITY rides on Native Runtime mechanics (disallowedTools, managed-settings.d/, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}) rather than bespoke runtime code.

primitives.name: Layer-1 RBAC Policy
primitives.description: Per-project managed-settings.d/50-palantir-mini.json fragment declares permission allowlists (which tools, which paths, which MCP servers). Layer 1 only — no object-level markings, no cascading, no dynamic role escalation. Scoped to cross-project need.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-04: governs WHICH TOOLS AND PATHS an agent may touch. Classification: SECURITY.
primitives.v0Scope: Only Layer 1. Markings/ObjectSecurity/PermissionsCascade explicitly deferred per decision-log projectOntologyScope.SECURITY.primitives (listed as 'Layer 1 RBAC, CapabilityToken, PermissionsCascade' — ontologist narrows to Layer-1 RBAC + CapabilityToken for v0, defers PermissionsCascade).

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — RBAC matches common security vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one fragment schema used across all 3 projects
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new permissions added via new fragment entries
designPrinciples.PECS: N/A — policy declarations, not generic types

forwardPropPath.chain: schemas/ontology/policies/rbac.ts -> codegen -> managed-settings.d/50-palantir-mini.json per project -> Claude Code runtime enforces on every tool call

backwardPropPath.chain: permission denial in runtime -> hook observation -> policy_denied event (NEW variant — consider adding to EventEnvelope union) -> refinement
gaps: policy_denied event variant not yet in 10-variant union; needs addition if Layer-1 RBAC is to participate in LEARN backward propagation

primitives.name: SubmissionCriteria (gate semantics)
primitives.description: Pre-flight gate that blocks commits unless business-logic conditions pass. This is the SECURITY dual of prim-logic-07 (computational). LOGIC evaluates pass/fail; SECURITY is the gate that USES the evaluation to permit or deny the commit. Intentional dual modeling per DC5 DDD.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-04: 'under what conditions an action is permitted' -> SECURITY. The evaluator is LOGIC (it computes). The gate is SECURITY (it governs).
primitives.dualModelingRationale: DC5 DDD: the same real-world concept can have different semantic homes depending on which slice you name. The computation goes in LOGIC so it can be tested/replayed without commit. The gate semantics goes in SECURITY so policy review can target it directly without digging through function bodies. See ontologist.md line 222: 'Do NOT collapse SECURITY and LEARN concepts into D/L/A.'

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — 'submission criteria' matches Palantir ACTION.MU-14 vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PARTIAL — intentional duality with prim-logic-07 is duplicate surface area but single truth source (computation lives once)
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new gate types added without modifying existing

forwardPropPath.chain: schemas/ontology/policies/submission-criteria.ts -> codegen -> commit_edits MCP gate -> evaluator invocation (LOGIC) -> pass|fail -> commit | submission_criteria_failed event

backwardPropPath.chain: submission_criteria_failed event -> replay_lineage filter by failedConstraints -> gate refinement

primitives.name: CapabilityToken (vault pattern)
primitives.description: Secrets (API keys, signing keys, tokens) live in ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/vault/ — which SURVIVES plugin updates — and are referenced by capability token, never embedded in generated code. Mirrors Anthropic Managed Agents 'Vault proxy' pattern.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-04: governs WHO holds credentials and under what conditions -> SECURITY.
primitives.nativeRuntimeMechanism: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} env var per blueprint.md line 314 — this path survives plugin updates (unlike ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}). Codegen never emits secret values; it emits capability token references that runtime resolves through vault.

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — 'capability token' + 'vault' match Anthropic Managed Agents + Palantir SECURITY vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — single vault per plugin install, referenced by ID
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new capability types added via new vault entries

backwardPropPath.chain: vault access -> audit log (capability_accessed event — NEW variant needed) -> refinement

learn.name: Learn Domain (What Feedback Is Recorded)
learn.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: Does this capture WHAT HAPPENED, WHY, and WHAT WAS LEARNED from it? -> LEARN.
learn.centralRole: LEARN is the foundational domain for palantir-mini. Everything else writes to events.jsonl. BackwardPropagation literally IS the LEARN domain being read and fed back to schemas/ontology/.

primitives.name: AppendOnlyEventLog (events.jsonl)
primitives.description: Per-project <project>/.palantir-mini/session/events.jsonl. Append-only. Immutable lines. Each line is a serialized EventEnvelope (prim-data-01). This IS the Anthropic Managed Agents 'Session' primitive, scoped per-project.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: events.jsonl IS the record of what happened, why, and what was learned. Classification: LEARN.
primitives.threeSourceConvergence: Independent convergence on the append-only event log from 3 references: (1) Anthropic Managed Agents engineering article 'Session = append-only log of everything that happened', (2) Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim, (3) OMC clawhip 'event routing to prevent context pollution' (760 stars, sister project of 28.9k-star OMC). Strongest external validation available without API.
primitives.source: decision-log RQ-01 + decision-log hypothesisProvisional + blueprint.md Gap fill 1/3

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — 'append-only event log' matches Anthropic + Palantir + OMC vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — single log per project serves as truth for ALL state (ontology, lineage, validation, codegen, phase gates)
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new event variants extend the discriminated union (see EventEnvelope OCP)
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — emit_event (producer) extends, replay_lineage (consumer) uses super via exhaustive visitor

backwardPropPath.chain: events.jsonl -> replay_lineage MCP handler -> 5-dim lineage graph -> drift_detected if ontology divergence -> ontology refinement proposal -> ontologist review -> schemas/ontology/ update

primitives.name: DecisionLineage5Dim
primitives.description: Palantir Decision Lineage 5 dimensions: WHEN, ATOP_WHICH, THROUGH_WHICH, BY_WHOM, WITH_WHAT. Captured on every EventEnvelope via EventEnvelopeBase. Enables root-cause analysis of ontology changes.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: captures WHY and BY_WHOM — the lineage IS the learning record. Classification: LEARN.

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — all 5 dimension names from Palantir
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — captured once in EventEnvelopeBase, inherited by all 10 variants
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new lineage dimensions (e.g. FOR_WHAT, UPON_WHAT) can be added to base without breaking variants

forwardPropPath.chain: hook observes tool call -> captures 5 dims -> populates EventEnvelopeBase -> events.jsonl

backwardPropPath.chain: replay_lineage(filter: {byWhom, atopWhich}) -> filtered event stream -> targeted refinement queries

primitives.name: EvidenceSufficiency
primitives.description: Meta-evaluation of whether a claim or scenario has sufficient evidence. Per docs/ontology-mapping-rules.md line 133: sufficient | partial | insufficient. Used by evaluator R9 gate.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: measures the QUALITY of accumulated knowledge. Classification: LEARN.

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS

forwardPropPath.chain: claim/scenario -> evaluator computes EvidenceSufficiency -> gate R9

backwardPropPath.chain: low EvidenceSufficiency -> researcher spawn for more evidence -> re-evaluation

primitives.name: LineageReplay
primitives.description: Deterministic replay of events.jsonl filtered by 5-dim lineage. Given a filter (e.g. 'all events byWhom.agentName=ontologist in the last 24h'), returns the ordered event stream. Foundation for backward propagation: lineage query -> pattern detection -> refinement.
primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: LineageReplay EXISTS to surface what was learned from past events. Classification: LEARN. NOTE: it is also a pure computation (no side effects) so it could classify as LOGIC. Ontologist rules: LEARN wins because the PURPOSE is learning, not reasoning.
primitives.classificationTiebreaker: SH-01 + SH-05: LineageReplay returns event streams (not Edits[]) so it is not ACTION. It computes (not commits) so it is not ACTION. It could be LOGIC (computation) or LEARN (audit surface). Ontologist rule: when a computation's SOLE PURPOSE is to surface audit trails for refinement, LEARN wins. This is consistent with ontologist.md line 36 which reserves LEARN for 'feedback, outcomes, refinement recording'.
primitives.source: decision-log RQ-04 replay_lineage MCP tool + blueprint.md Gap fill 2 line 386

designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — one replay primitive serves all backward propagation queries
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new filter types added without modifying core replay

primitives.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: drift IS the learning signal — 'ontology said X, runtime did Y, delta is what needs refinement.' Classification: LEARN.

gaps: Step 4 (refinement proposal persistence) is non-durable in v0 — lives only in SendMessage + decision-log append. Pipeline should decide whether proposals merit their own state file or if events.jsonl (with a new refinement_proposed variant) suffices.
Step 5 (ontologist review decision artifact) should itself be a LEARN-domain event. Consider adding review_decision variant to EventEnvelope union in post-prototype phase.

DDD.findings: All primitive names match Palantir / Anthropic Managed Agents / OSDK 2.0 domain vocabulary exactly. 'EditFunction', 'SubmissionCriteria', 'EventEnvelope', 'DecisionLineage', 'ObjectTypeRid', 'Tier-1/Tier-2 Action' — zero translation required. The cross-project scope (kosmos/palantir-math/mathcrew) forces shared vocabulary by construction.

DRY.findings: Single ontology declaration in ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/ feeds codegen for all 3 projects — no duplication. Single EventEnvelope discriminated union hosts 10 event types without per-type envelope boilerplate. Single AtomicCommit primitive serves Tier-1 fast path and Tier-2 slow path. One replay_lineage MCP handler serves all backward propagation queries.

OCP.findings: Discriminated unions enable Open/Closed extension: new EventEnvelope variants add cases, never modify existing. New ObjectTypes/LinkTypes/ActionTypes added as new declarations. New validation phase evaluators added without modifying existing. Codegen templates extended, never mutated.

PECS.findings: Producer Extends, Consumer Super applied at every generic boundary: EditFunction<Params, Edits extends OntologyEdit[]> (Edits is producer-extends), Reducer<In, Out> (In is consumer-super), Replay<Filter, Event> (Filter is consumer-super), Action<F extends EditFunction<any,any>> (F is producer-extends). Exhaustive visitor pattern on EventEnvelope type tag replaces switch statements per OSDK 2.0 convention.

gapsResolved.gap: StaleObject optimistic concurrency in append-only model — does sequence number in EventEnvelope base work as version vector?
gapsResolved.resolution: YES. EventEnvelopeBase.sequence is a monotonic counter per project events.jsonl. On append, the plugin reads the tail of events.jsonl (under proper-lockfile mutex), verifies sequence = lastSequence + 1, then appends. A concurrent writer that holds a stale lastSequence will fail the check and retry with the current tail. This is functionally equivalent to Palantir StaleObject optimistic concurrency: the 'version' is the last observed sequence; a conflict is a mismatched sequence at append time. Zero-dep fs.mkdir lockfile provides the mutual exclusion, so the check-and-append is atomic.
gapsResolved.forwardPropImpact: codegen must emit the sequence-check wrapper in commit_edits MCP handler; this is a generator obligation, not hand-written runtime
gapsResolved.backwardPropImpact: sequence gaps in events.jsonl are themselves a drift_detected signal — indicates write failures or external tampering

gapsResolved.gap: Edit function rollback via compensating events (append-only means no delete)
gapsResolved.resolution: Resolved via compensating events. Append-only means you never DELETE a committed event; you APPEND a compensating inverse event. Pattern: if edit_committed event E1 added Spacekid obj-123 with name='Alice', rollback emits a new edit_committed event E2 whose appliedEdits contains Edits.Object.delete(obj-123). The snapshot reducer folds both events and arrives at 'obj-123 does not exist.' This is the same model Palantir OSDK 2.0 uses for Action revert. The sequence counter continues monotonically; no time travel.
gapsResolved.forwardPropImpact: codegen emits an invert(Edits[]) -> Edits[] helper for every ActionType; this is a reducer-level obligation
gapsResolved.backwardPropImpact: replay_lineage can trace (E1, E2) pairs via shared metadata in withWhat.reasoning='rollback_of:E1' — enables root-cause analysis of rollback patterns

gapsResolved.resolution: Per blueprint.md decision-log constraint 'Claude primary writer, Codex/Gemini consume schemas/ontology/ as readers via filesystem,' there is only ONE writer runtime (Claude Code). Codex and Gemini are READ-ONLY consumers via filesystem. This sidesteps multi-writer coordination entirely. If Claude Code runs in parallel sessions against the same schemas/, the per-project events.jsonl lockfile + sequence check (gap-01) handles that layer; the schemas/ directory itself is under normal git, so parallel Claude Code sessions coordinate via git branches like any TypeScript project. NO NEW MECHANISM NEEDED for v0.
gapsResolved.forwardPropImpact: no codegen obligation; git handles it

gapsResolved.gap: Concrete EventEnvelope TypeScript schema (from source-map.json gapsFilledConcrete)
gapsResolved.resolution: Fully resolved by blueprint.md Gap fill 1 (lines 321-376). 10-variant discriminated union with EventEnvelopeBase holding 5-dim lineage + sequence counter. All 5 domains touch EventEnvelope: DATA owns the type, LOGIC computes payload via EditFunction, ACTION triggers append via AtomicCommit, SECURITY governs who may emit via agent allowlists, LEARN reads via replay_lineage. One primitive, five-domain touchpoint.

gapsDeferred.gap: Semantic vs Kinetic 2-layer terminology — where do LOGIC functions fit?
gapsDeferred.rationale: Flagged in blueprint.md open questions line 470. Not required for v0 ontology. Ontologist position: in palantir-mini terms, 'semantic' = DATA primitives (ObjectType, PropertyType), 'kinetic' = ACTION primitives (Tier-1/Tier-2/AtomicCommit). LOGIC sits BETWEEN — it is the reasoning layer that connects semantic to kinetic. This is consistent with ontologist.md transition zone rule. Full Palantir terminology harmonization deferred to post-prototype phase.

gapsDeferred.gap: Workflow Lineage vs Decision Lineage relationship
gapsDeferred.rationale: Flagged in blueprint.md open question line 476. Both are official Palantir products. For v0 palantir-mini ontology we model Decision Lineage 5-dim only (per DecisionLineage5Dim primitive). Workflow Lineage is a Foundry-scale concept not mapped to events.jsonl yet. Simulator should consider if the 11-dim scenario matrix needs a WorkflowLineage dimension.

conflicts_detected.description: decision-log.json lists InterfaceType and LinkType under BackendOntology.DATA.primitives, but ontologist.md Transition Zone rule (line 68) classifies them as LOGIC.
conflicts_detected.resolution: Reclassified to LOGIC per ontologist authority. decision-log listing is tolerated as 'scope membership' not 'semantic home'. Documented in prim-logic-02 and prim-logic-03 classificationNote fields. No change to decision-log.json required — it documents scope, world-model.json documents semantic classification.

conflicts_detected.description: SubmissionCriteria is modeled in BOTH LOGIC (prim-logic-07) and SECURITY (prim-sec-02) domains.
conflicts_detected.resolution: Intentional dual modeling per DC5 DDD. The COMPUTATION is LOGIC (evaluates constraint classes, returns pass/fail — no side effects). The GATE SEMANTICS is SECURITY (policy that governs whether a commit is permitted). Both point at the same runtime evaluator; the duality lives at the classification layer. Documented in prim-sec-02.dualModelingRationale.
conflicts_detected.severity: N/A — not a conflict, intentional design

contradictionsFromSourceMap.note: The task briefing indicates source-map.json contains 6 contradictions including one HIGH severity. The on-disk source-map.json (Apr 12, photonicRealism session) does not contain the palantir-mini research output — those corrections are captured in palantir-mini-blueprint.md 'Research corrections' section (lines 271-306).
contradictionsFromSourceMap.highSeverityContradiction: Plugin agents CANNOT define hooks/mcpServers/permissionMode (blueprint.md Correction 1, HIGH severity). Resolution: all hooks live at plugin level hooks/hooks.json. Agents declare tool allowlists + disallowedTools only. Hooks use matchers to target specific agents. This is enforced in the ACTION domain via prim-action-04 (ActionExecutorAgent) — all hook logic goes in plugin level, agents only own their tool surface.
contradictionsFromSourceMap.mediumSeverityContradiction_1: PreToolUse uses hookSpecificOutput, not top-level decision (blueprint.md Correction 2). Resolution: documented in prim-logic-08 ValidationPhaseEvaluator mapping — phase-to-hook mapping must use hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision for PreToolUse, top-level decision for PostToolUse/Stop/etc.
contradictionsFromSourceMap.mediumSeverityContradiction_2: Plugin file locations standardized (blueprint.md Correction 3). Resolution: monitors live in monitors/monitors.json (separate file), not in plugin.json. No ontology impact; architecture detail only.

referentialIntegrity.transitionZoneAudit: LinkType, InterfaceType, DerivedProperty, Reducer all verified as LOGIC (not DATA) per ontologist.md line 68-73.

nextAgent.handoffSummary: World model is READY. 28 primitives across all 5 domains. 2 primary hypotheses already seeded in decision-log.json: H-A (Append-only event log foundation) vs H-B (Snapshot-based StaleObject optimistic concurrency). Ontologist recommends simulator focus the 11-dim scenario matrix on these 3 cuts: (1) H-A vs H-B across base/best/worst/adversarial — adversarial is the highest-signal scenario because gap-01 resolution relies on sequence-check correctness; (2) 3-phase vs 6-phase validation pipeline trade-off; (3) Layer-1 RBAC adequacy vs v0 SECURITY deferral of Markings/PermissionsCascade. The transition zone reclassifications (LinkType/InterfaceType -> LOGIC) should NOT be re-debated; they are ontologist authority.
